course_description:
This course includes 120 placement hours and will continue to build on the midwifery foundational skills learned in semester 1. In this semester, there is a focus on the principles of medication administration in the mother and neonate. Safety in drug administration as well as observational techniques for the mother and neonate required when drugs are utilised will be learned. Students will develop a comprehensive understanding of the principles and mechanisms of normal labour and birth and conduct of the care and support of a woman through a normal labour and birth. Non-pharmacological methods of pain relief in pregnancy, labour and birth are also considered. Students will be assessed for clinical competence and continue to maintain a portfolio of evidence in the acquisition of Midwifery Practice Experiences (MPEs), in varied maternity related settings.

This course will provide students with ongoing opportunities to develop their clinical practice skills. The focus of the semester will surround medication administration and the safe practice when performing this task. Students will also build upon their role in supporting the woman during labour and birth.

Throughout the semester, clinical learning experiences will be planned to integrate with the theoretical concepts covered in the companion courses. These experiences will occur throughout the semester at your clinical placement site. Students will be rostered to attend either morning, afternoon or night duty shifts for eight (8)ﾠhours duration, and will complete fifteen (15) shifts during the semester. Students must be aware that when they are attending clinical placement, they are there as representatives of the School of Nursing, Midwifery and Social Work of the University of Queensland, therefore, professional deportment and professional behaviour is essential. In addition to their clinical placement, students will have planned clinical based learning (CBL) sessions in the laboratories with a Lecturer. In these sessions, they will learn specific skills that relate to the course, which they may then implementﾠunder supervision ﾠ in their clinical placement.

Throughout the semester students are required to submit evidence of progressﾠduringﾠplacement according to assessment and learning activity requirements as outlined in Sections 6 and 8. If further evidence is required, students will be advised accordingly. Students who do not achieve/maintain clinical performance commensurate with learning and experience at any point in the semester may be placed on a practice learning plan by the Course Coordinator in consultation with the Practice Education Manager (Midwifery) / Clinical placement facilitator for ongoing support with the development of skills and clinical performance. If fitness to practice and safety concerns are raised, students may be withdrawn from the clinical placement at the discretion of the Practice Education Manager (Midwifery) / Program Lead, and in consultation with the Head of School.

course_aims: The course aims to provide students with a foundation of midwifery skills through clinical practice gained in different midwifery settings. This course will continue to emphasise to students the Nursing and Midwifery Board of Australia (NMBA) Midwife Standards of Practice (2018) and how these standards provide a framework for the care of women and babies.ﾠ

learning_outcomes.description: Explore the role of the midwife in providing woman centred inter-professional care including those requiring drug therapy during pregnancy, birth, and the postnatal period.

Exemplify and utilise available technologies to fulfil clinical requirements for attainment of midwifery practice and continuity of care experiences through recruitment, partnership, and development of effective relationships with women and the healthcare team.

Explain the legal and professional responsibilities, and the frameworks for safe midwifery practice including knowledge of scope of practice and the need to accept accountability and responsibility for safe drug administration.

Explain and demonstrate beginning level midwifery practice skills including monitoring and care of the woman and her baby's health, wellbeing, and responses to implemented care including administration of drugs in pregnancy, labour and birth, and the postpartum period.

Interpret how personal beliefs and the philosophy of woman centred care reflect continued enhancement of ethical decision making in the context of physical, psychological, and culturally responsive midwifery practice.

The due date for the MedSafe assessment is Friday of week three. 3/08/2024 - 9/08/2024 The due date for the Skill and Mid-point AMSAT assessments are based upon your clinical placement start date with the assessments being due by the Friday of Week 6 ( approximately 60 hours) of your placement. 30/08/2024 - 4/12/2024 All requirements must be submitted and met to complete this assessment which contributes to passing the course.
assessment_details.other_conditions: Student specific, Work integrated learning. See the conditions definitions
assessment_details.task_description: This assessment task comprises of three (3) parts which will examine and extend your preparedness for clinical placement and development in the first half of the semester. Part 1 – Medication safety This assessment task requires the student to undertake an online assessment on medication safety as well as completion of a drug calculations assessment. Students must receive a 100% pass mark to be able to pass this assessment. Further instructions can be found in the assessment task and via the BlackBoard site . Part 2 - Skill Assessment This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice, and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. Part 3 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW1105 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT) The Mid-point AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife. Completion due date is mid placement (approx. week 6) - complete Standards 1, 2, and 3 only Please access the task description in the blackboard site for more information. Read full task description Hide full task description
assessment_details.submission_guidelines:
Students are required to upload their completed Med+Safe certificates to the companion blackboard site under Assessments.
Students are to complete their Skills and Mid-point AMSAT via their ePAD.
Deferral or extension
You may be able to apply for an extension .
The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.
Applying for an extension
Extension requests are submitted online via my.UQ. Extension requests received in any other way will not be approved.
Information about applying for an extension can be found on my.UQ .
Extension guidelines
Extension requests should be submitted prior to the assessment deadline. Extension requests must state the due date and the due time of the assessment. Requests for extensions received after the assessment item submission due date may not be approved. Late requests must include evidence of the reasons for the late request. The extension granted should be in proportion to the period of illness or disruption caused by the exceptional circumstances and will typically be no more than the specified maximum extension length. Students on a Student Access Plan (SAP) with a clause on extension requests must still adhere to the extensions request policy outlined. Students need to attach the SAP and a note outlining their current situation with the online extension application. A maximum of 7 days will be granted on the basis of an SAP. An extension request beyond 7 days will require additional supporting information as this request will not be considered on the basis of the SAP, but rather additional events (whether they relate to the circumstances of the SAP or not).
Outcome of application
Extension requests are generally processed within 2 working days. Students can see the outcome of their application by logging in to my.UQ.
assessment_details.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension .

assessment_details.task_description: This assessment requires you to demonstrate successful completion of clinical placement. There are three (3) parts to this assessment: Part 1 - Skill Assessment This assessment requires demonstration to perform a range of skills required for competent and safe midwifery practice. The aim of this task is to actively seek feedback about your practice, and evaluate your own performance to plan for improvements. Part 2 - Demonstrated capability in clinical practice commensurate with the MIDW1105 Course Learning Objectives evidenced by completion and submission of the Australian Midwifery Standards Assessment Tool (AMSAT) The Final AMSAT must be completed in Myprogress ePAD and signed by your Practice Educator/ Clinical Facilitator OR Preceptor Midwife. Completion due date is at the end of your placement (approx. week 13) - complete Standards 1, 2, and 3 only Part 3 - Completion and upload of required Midwifery Practice Experiences (MPE) including Continuity of Care Experiences (CoCE). Please access the task description in the blackboard site for more information. Read full task description Hide full task description

The maximum extension allowed is 28 days. Extensions are given in multiples of 24 hours.

policies_and_procedures: University policies and procedures apply to all aspects of student life. As a UQ student, you must comply with University-wide and program-specific requirements, including the: Student Code of Conduct Policy Student Integrity and Misconduct Policy and Procedure Assessment Procedure Examinations Procedure Reasonable Adjustments - Students Policy and Procedure Learn more about UQ policies on my.UQ and the Policy and Procedure Library . You'll also need to be aware of the following policies and procedures while completing this course: Placement Working with Children Fitness to Practise Immunisation Course guidelines Students must comply with the Clinical Placement Shift and Rostering Guidelines, Mandatory preparation requirements and Scope of Practice documents. Refer to the Clinical Placements Tab in the companion Blackboard site for details. Learning activities
